hypothesis: Replacing NN-BAX’s random path-point acquisition with an ensemble-uncertainty–guided policy that queries the simulator at the predicted highest-energy image with maximal predictive variance will reduce simulator evaluations by at least 50% relative to the current NN-BAX to reach identical fmax and MAEi thresholds on DFT-based surface reaction NEB tasks, without increasing energy-barrier error beyond 0.05 eV.
